content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

October 19, 1998

Charles H. Bell, Jr.
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk
455 Capitol Mall, Suite 801
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-98-250

Dear Mr. Bell:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Donald Murphy regarding the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ We received your request on October 13, 1998, for expedited advice regarding a meeting to be held on October 17, 1998. Due to the impending statewide election and other work in progress, we apologize that we were unable to provide you with formal written advice before October 17, 1998. We plan to issue you a letter as soon as practicable pending receipt of the "revised advice request" referred to in this advice request.

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Marte Castaños
SGC:MC:ls     Staff Counsel, Legal Division

_________________________________

¹ Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 - 18995, of the California Code of Regulations.

I:\Advice Ltrs\98250.wpd

sections.question_synthetic: The document does not contain a substantive legal question, as this is a preliminary response letter indicating that formal written advice will be provided pending receipt of a revised advice request.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC was unable to provide formal written advice prior to the October 17, 1998 meeting due to workload constraints, and will issue a letter as soon as practicable once a revised advice request is received.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No substantive question or conclusion sections present. Document appears to be a holding/acknowledgment letter rather than a complete advice letter. The actual legal question and FPPC's conclusion are not included in this document; they are expected in a subsequent 'revised advice request' and response.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC was unable to provide formal written advice prior to the October 17, 1998 meeting due to workload constraints, and will issue a letter as soon as practicable once a revised advice request is received.

embedding.summary: This is a preliminary response letter from the FPPC General Counsel to attorney Charles H. Bell, Jr. regarding an expedited advice request on behalf of Donald Murphy concerning Political Reform Act provisions, noting that formal written advice will follow upon receipt of a revised request.
